source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/250623 포터블 태국TFDA 등록 요청서류 - 이창민 팀장님/TFDA Form/TFDA on Handheld/6B36DF58A224B10B21C735174D80AE167108C57A_Non-IVD_F_08_Essential_Principles_of_Safety_and_Performance_of_Medical Devices.docx

document.title: Essential Principles of Safety and Performance of Medical Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Checklist of essential principles for safety and performance of medical devices.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to document whether essential safety and performance principles apply to a medical device, the method of conformity, and the specific supporting documents.
keyPoints: Medical devices must not compromise patient, user, or other person safety when used as intended.
Product owners must identify hazards, reduce risks through safe design, apply protective measures, and inform users of residual risks.
Medical devices require appropriate clinical evidence and a clinical evaluation.

Design and manufacturing requirements address materials, contamination, infection, environment, measuring functions, radiation, software, energy sources, mechanical risks, supplied energy or substances, active implantables, self-testing or self-administration, and product information.
Clinical investigations on human subjects are to be carried out in accordance with the spirit of the Helsinki Declaration.

entities.name: Medical devices
entities.evidence: Medical devices shall be designed and manufactured in such a way that, when used under the conditions and for the purposes intended... they will not compromise the clinical condition or the safety of patients

entities.name: Product owner
entities.evidence: The solutions adopted by the product owner for the design and manufacture of the medical devices shall conform to safety principles

entities.name: Helsinki Declaration
entities.evidence: Clinical investigations on human subjects shall be carried out in accordance with the spirit of the Helsinki Declaration.

entities.name: IVD medical device
entities.evidence: This may not apply to certain IVD medical device if the activity of the virus and other transmissible agent are integral to the intended purpose of the IVD medical device

concepts.name: Essential Principles of Safety and Performance
concepts.pageTarget: Essential Principles of Safety and Performance of Medical Devices
concepts.evidence: Essential Principles of Safety and Performance of Medical Devices

concepts.name: Risk reduction by safe design
concepts.evidence: eliminate or reduce risks as far as reasonably practicable through inherently safe design and manufacture

concepts.name: Clinical evidence and clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: Medical devices shall require clinical evidence, appropriate for the use and classification of the medical device... A clinical evaluation shall be conducted.

concepts.name: Infection and microbial contamination
concepts.pageTarget: Medical Device Infection and Microbial Contamination

concepts.name: Sterile medical devices
concepts.evidence: Medical devices delivered in a sterile state shall be designed, manufactured and packed to ensure that they remain sterile

concepts.name: Medical devices with a diagnostic or measuring function
concepts.pageTarget: Medical Device Diagnostic and Measuring Functions
concepts.evidence: Medical devices with a measuring function shall be designed and manufactured in such a way as to provide sufficient accuracy, precision and stability

concepts.name: Software validation
concepts.evidence: the software shall be validated according to the state of the art taking into account the principles of development lifecycle, risk management, validation and verification

concepts.name: Information supplied by the product owner
concepts.pageTarget: Medical Device Labeling and Instructions for Use
concepts.evidence: The following information shall be provided with a medical device, having regard to the training and knowledge of potential users of the medical device

concepts.name: Clinical investigation
concepts.evidence: Clinical investigations on human subjects shall be carried out in accordance with the spirit of the Helsinki Declaration.

evidence.claim: The source is an Essential Principles checklist for safety and performance of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: Essential Principles of Safety and Performance of Medical Devices
evidence.pageTarget: Essential Principles of Safety and Performance of Medical Devices

evidence.claim: The checklist captures applicability, method of conformity, and identity of specific documents for each essential principle.
evidence.evidenceText: Applicable to the device? Method of Conformity Identity of Specific Documents

evidence.claim: Medical devices must be designed and manufactured so intended use does not compromise patient, user, or other person safety.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical devices shall be designed and manufactured in such a way that, when used under the conditions and for the purposes intended... they will not compromise the clinical condition or the safety of patients, or the safety and health of users

evidence.claim: Product owners must identify hazards and associated risks from intended use and foreseeable misuse.
evidence.evidenceText: identify any hazard and associated risk arising from the use of the medical device for its intended purpose, and any foreseeable misuse of the medical device

evidence.claim: Product owners must eliminate or reduce risks through inherently safe design and manufacture as far as reasonably practicable.
evidence.evidenceText: eliminate or reduce risks as far as reasonably practicable through inherently safe design and manufacture

evidence.claim: Medical devices must achieve the performance intended by the product owner.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical devices shall achieve the performance intended by the product owner

evidence.claim: Medical devices require clinical evidence appropriate for use and classification, and a clinical evaluation must be conducted.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical devices shall require clinical evidence, appropriate for the use and classification of the medical device... A clinical evaluation shall be conducted.

evidence.claim: Material selection must consider toxicity, flammability, chemical and physical properties, compatibility with tissues and fluids, and material properties such as hardness, wear, and fatigue strength.
evidence.evidenceText: the choice of materials used, particularly as regards toxicity and, where appropriate, flammability... compatibility between the materials used and biological tissues, cells, body fluids, and specimens

evidence.claim: Medical devices and manufacturing processes must eliminate or reduce infection risk as far as reasonably practicable and appropriate.
evidence.evidenceText: The medical devices and manufacturing processes shall be designed in such a way as to eliminate or to reduce as far as reasonably practicable and appropriate the risk of infection
evidence.pageTarget: Medical Device Infection and Microbial Contamination

evidence.claim: Sterile medical devices must remain sterile when placed on the market and under specified transport and storage conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical devices delivered in a sterile state shall be designed, manufactured and packed to ensure that they remain sterile when placed on the market and remain sterile, under the transport and storage conditions indicated by the product owner.

evidence.claim: Medical devices with measuring functions must provide sufficient accuracy, precision, and stability for their intended purpose.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical devices with a measuring function shall be designed and manufactured in such a way as to provide sufficient accuracy, precision and stability for their intended purpose
evidence.pageTarget: Medical Device Diagnostic and Measuring Functions

evidence.claim: Software in or as a medical device must be validated according to the state of the art, considering development lifecycle, risk management, validation, and verification.
evidence.evidenceText: the software shall be validated according to the state of the art taking into account the principles of development lifecycle, risk management, validation and verification

evidence.claim: Information supplied with a medical device must identify the device, identify the product owner, and explain safe use.
evidence.evidenceText: information identifying the medical device; information identifying the product owner of the medical device; information explaining how to use the medical device safely
evidence.pageTarget: Medical Device Labeling and Instructions for Use

evidence.claim: Clinical investigations on human subjects must be carried out in accordance with the spirit of the Helsinki Declaration.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical investigations on human subjects shall be carried out in accordance with the spirit of the Helsinki Declaration.

reviewFlags.reason: Key metadata fields are blank in the source text, including EP Checklist control number, Product Owner Name, Product Name, prepared by, and approved by.
No document date, revision, or version is present in the source text.
Authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly stated in the source text; authoritySignal indicates FDA and the path references TFDA, but the document body does not identify an issuing authority.
Source text contains duplicated or inconsistent formatting in clause 2 and repeated clinical investigation wording, suggesting preprocessing or template quality issues.
File name indicates Non-IVD, but several clauses include IVD medical device exceptions; applicability should be confirmed for the target device.
